exemplar.title: M25 #2: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #2: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Clear, coherent links to the prescribed title and both areas of knowledge
Effective, discipline-specific examples (Guernica, Nazi âdegenerate artâ, Soviet Marxism collapse, cognitive revolution) tightly integrated into the argument
Sustained focus on the question of fragility versus reverence throughout the essay
weaknesses: Evaluation of counter-claims is only hinted at and lacks depth
Conceptual imprecision around key terms (ideology vs. knowledge; fragility) leaves some arguments underdeveloped
Conclusion and thesis roadmap are largely descriptive rather than offering critical insight or implications
feedback.calculationMethod:
Holistic AI evaluation: The examinerâs overall mark of 6/10 reflects a strong achievement in Criterion A (understanding the knowledge question) and Criterion C (use of relevant, well-chosen examples), balanced by more modest performance in Criterion B (depth of analysis/evaluation), Criterion D (clarity of structure and thesis roadmap) and Criterion E (critical engagement and independent TOK reflection).  

On reverse distribution, the essay justifies full credit for understanding and example-use (2â+â2), but only minimal credit for analysis (1), organization (1) and engagement (0), summing exactly to the prescribed total of 6.

annotations.comment: The student directly addresses the prescribed title in the header, demonstrating immediate focus on the question to be explored.
The introduction of behaviorism would benefit from clearer linkage back to the title question on fragility, to maintain coherence.
The description of the cognitive revolution concretely shows how methodological advances can rejuvenate a field, reinforcing resilience amid fragility.
The summary of behaviorism oversimplifies its methodology; specifying landmark experiments would strengthen the argument.
Effective connection of the artworkâs universal symbolism back to the essayâs central question on fragility versus reverence.
The student defines key terms but could enhance precision by explaining what constitutes 'fragility' conceptually before moving to examples.
The student conflates ideology with knowledge; consider distinguishing between political ideology and epistemic claims to avoid conceptual ambiguity.
The closing sentence reaffirms the argumentâs trajectory but could be refined to offer a more conclusive insight rather than mere restatement.
The student cites authoritative sources but could strengthen analysis by discussing how these works shape different perspectives on the chosen examples.
The introduction provides a general definition of 'revered knowledge' but lacks a clear thesis statement outlining how the argument will be structured across both areas of knowledge.
The essay mentions analysis âfrom the knowersâ perspectiveâ but does not develop this epistemologically, missing an opportunity to explore TOK frameworks.
The example of Nazi âdegenerate artâ is a compelling illustration of how socio-political forces can render revered artistic knowledge vulnerable.
The link between political suppression and knowledge fragility is asserted but not fully unpacked; explain why dependency on social structures constitutes epistemic fragility.
The discussion of Guernica effectively traces the evolution of its interpretation, showing awareness of how cultural contexts reshape reverence.
Referencing in-class TOK discussion reduces the impression of independent inquiry; cite scholarly commentary or primary sources to substantiate claims.
The final sentence hints at a thesis but remains incomplete; the student should present a concise roadmap of the essayâs arguments to guide the reader.
The student successfully contrasts the arts and human sciences, demonstrating effective cross-AOK integration.
The analysis could be deepened by considering alternative viewpoints, such as how art historians have reconstructed suppressed movements despite political censorship.
The student relies on a single secondary source (Barron, 1991); incorporating primary accounts or visual analysis of surviving works would strengthen the argument.
The transition to the discussion of Marxism ends abruptly without completing the exploration of its foundational theories; ensure full development of each example.
The analysis of the Soviet collapse vividly illustrates how practical application can undermine theoretical frameworks, reinforcing the fragility argument.
Including an evaluation of Marxismâs continued theoretical value in academia would add nuance and demonstrate reflexivity.
The conclusion synthesizes main points but remains largely descriptive; deeper evaluation of implications would elevate the analysis to critical insight.
The claim that fragility allows longevity is insightful but requires justification through a specific comparative example to avoid abstraction.
The References header appears, but IB guidelines require a consistent referencing style (e.g., APA); ensure compliance with TOK criteria.
The essay maintains sustained focus on the title, with clear, coherent links to both the arts and human sciences throughout.

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of

Arguments are

examples.
There is some

different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
